channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Israel faces shortage of missile interceptors as Iranian attacks continue
item.description: Israel is reportedly facing a critical shortage of ballistic missile interceptors as it contends with ongoing Iranian attacks. US officials have confirmed that Israel entered the conflict with deplete...

item.title: Philippine Women’s Football Team Falls to Japan but Eyes World Cup Play-in
item.description: The Philippine women’s national football team, the Filipinas, faced a tough challenge against Japan in the quarterfinals of the 2026 AFC Women’s Asian Cup. Despite holding their own for over 40 minute...

item.title: Dennis Busulwa Ssebo and his role in making Singeli music national
item.description: Dennis Busulwa, commonly called Ssebo in Tanzania’s media circles, passed away on March 11, 2026. Known as a quiet yet powerful force behind the scenes, he served as Director of Operations and Busines...

item.title: Academic Challenges in Studying Unidentified Aerial Phenomena in the United States
item.description: Despite growing government attention on unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP) in the United States, academic research on the topic faces significant barriers. In February 2026, former President Donal...

item.title: Dom Young scores second try for Newcastle Knights against Manly
item.description: In the latest NRL clash, Dom Young made a significant impact for the Newcastle Knights by scoring their second try against Manly. The match showcased Young's strength and agility as he powered over to...

item.title: Lib Dems call for a fully independent UK nuclear deterrent
item.description: The leader of the Liberal Democrats, Sir Ed Davey, has urged the UK government to establish a completely independent nuclear deterrent, aiming to end the country's reliance on the United States. Altho...

item.title: Ukraine seeks to maintain U.S. support despite Middle East conflict
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has expressed concern that ongoing U.S. involvement in the Iran conflict might divert attention from Ukraine’s war with Russia. Speaking to journalists, includi...

item.title: ANC-linked companies receive millions for water supply despite lacking tankers
item.description: A recent investigation has uncovered that two companies connected to prominent ANC figures in Tshwane received roughly R31.7 million to supply water via tankers, even though neither company owns a sin...

item.title: Israeli airstrike in Lebanon kills 12 medical staff as peace talks are proposed
item.description: In a troubling escalation in Lebanon, an overnight Israeli airstrike on a healthcare centre in Borj Qalaouiya resulted in the deaths of at least twelve medical personnel, including doctors, nurses, an...

item.title: Ogun State Prepares Gateway International Airport for First Hajj Flights
item.description: Ogun State don enter new era for air travel as dem dey prepare Gateway International Airport for first international operations, starting with Hajj flights to Saudi Arabia. Before now, people wey dey ...

item.title: Trump Signals Potential Further Military Strikes on Iran’s Key Oil Hub Amid Escalating Tensions
item.description: The tensions in West Asia are escalating as United States President Donald Trump stated that American forces might strike targets on Iran’s Kharg Island oil export hub 'just for fun'. Trump claimed th...

item.title: UN praises Tanzania for setting up independent commission on October 2025 incidents
item.description: The United Nations (UN) Special Rapporteur, Gina Romero, has commended Tanzania for forming an independent Commission of Inquiry to look into the October 29th, 2025 incidents surrounding the general e...

item.title: David Patrikarakos: From law to documenting conflict and disaster across the world
item.description: David Patrikarakos recounts his unlikely transformation from a lawyer into a war correspondent whose career has taken him from the Middle East to Ukraine and Los Angeles. Originally trained as a lawye...

item.title: Trump Administration Declines Diplomatic Efforts to Negotiate Iran Ceasefire Amid Escalating Conflict
item.description: The Trump administration has reportedly rejected diplomatic attempts by Middle Eastern allies to initiate ceasefire talks with Iran following a two-week-long conflict that began with a significant U.S...

item.title: Mudavadi Travels to Russia to Discuss Safety and Welfare of Kenyans Abroad
item.description: Prime Cabinet Secretary Musalia Mudavadi has departed for Russia to hold discussions with Russian authorities over the safety of Kenyans residing there. The two-day trip aims to address concerns over ...

item.title: 19-year-old pleads guilty to impaired driving causing death and dangerous driving in Calgary crashes
item.description: Kane Burrows, 19, has pleaded guilty to multiple charges, including impaired driving causing death, impaired driving causing injury, and dangerous driving, following two separate crashes in Calgary in...

item.title: PS Omollo condemns Migori police station attack that left 22 officers injured
item.description: The article reports on a worrying incident in Migori County where a large group of residents stormed a local police station, leaving 22 police officers injured during the confrontation. It explains th...

item.title: Four politicians express interest in joining VP Duterte’s 2028 Senate slate
item.description: In a recent statement, Vice President Sara Duterte confirmed that four politicians have approached her expressing their desire to be part of her prospective senatorial slate for the 2028 national elec...

item.title: Barnaby Joyce urges fuel rationing to protect food supply amid petrol shortages
item.description: Barnaby Joyce, the One Nation MP and former Nationals leader, has proposed fuel rationing in Australia to ensure the continued supply of food during the ongoing petrol shortages. Speaking on ABC Insid...

item.title: 15-year-old girl dies in Buckinghamshire motorbike collision, three teenagers arrested
item.description: A tragic collision occurred on the A355 in Buckinghamshire late on Friday evening, resulting in the death of a 15-year-old girl. The incident involved a motorbike and two other vehicles, a white Toyot...
